violations.text: Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. PIC notified they talked with staff. Staff person washed their hands. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Chicken (karaage) was observed held at 117 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Inspector observed rice acidification log not completed for the last 7 days when sushi rice was prepared. PIC notified and tested during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Various items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC relocated items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was 0 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quat sanitizer is 200 ppm. PIC refilled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC removed and placed in 3 bay sink to be washed and sanitized.. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025
Inspector observed ice wand being stored on an unclean surface. PIC sanitized equipment and wrapped it during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. PIC had staff mark menus during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Inspector noted that an air break has been installed on the sink; discussed air gap and provided RIDOH guidance for PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024
ROP fish was observed thawing in the prep unit with packaging intact and an internal temperature of 40 degrees. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

Various foods packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024
Cases of fryer oil were observed stored on the floor near the friers. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients in the cook like area. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC removed bowls during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

Utensils in the storage crocks are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the cafe area. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. Inspector provided thermo labels during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the low boy gaskets in the sushi prep area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

The sinks used for food preparation are lacking air gaps. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Stainer was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Raw animal foods (eggs, chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (milk, beef/pork) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Curry and dachi was observed held at 50-54 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of manager, curry and dachi was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as sushi) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Onigiri, packaged meals, and sushi were observed held at 45-80 degrees Fahrenheit in grab and go open air cases. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed into walk-in cooler to chill and cool. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Sheet pan of cooked pork was observed held at (50) degrees Fahrenheit in a prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked chicken, and curry were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Foods were separated into smaller containers and ice baths during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

Various foods were observed held at 44-80 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview of PIC, foods were recently prepared. Foods were placed into walk-in cooler to blast chill product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

Commercially packaged desserts were observed not marked with the date item was thawed. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

Sushi rice pH logs are not being maintained. Establishment to follow HACCP procedures including maintaining records. Acidification logs to be kept on file for inspector review. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, grab and go meals were placed hot cold holding units for retail sale. Foods were placed into walk-in cooler to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

At the time of inspection, ROP yellow tail was thawing with package intact. Reduced oxygen packaging fish that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment. Temperature was observed to be over 38 degrees Fahrenheit, PIC voluntarily disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

Sushi packaged in the food establishment are lacking all required information on food label. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients (including sub ingredients), the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

The door to the outside, located in the refuse area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

Single service items and food were observed stored on the floor. Foods observed in refuse trash room. Foods and single service items to be protected from sources of contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

Observed the refuse area to have a heavy accumulation of garbage and cardboard boxes. Area must be cleaned and maintained. More frequent pickups required or larger disposal vessels. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the trash room area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions for pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

The doors to the outside, located in the front and rear, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

Reach in freezer was observed at an ambient of 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Open air case displays were observed at ambient temperatures of 44 and 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

The cutting boards on the prep units are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the bottom shelving of the reach-in freezer and coils of walk-in refrigerator condenser have an accumulation of residue and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024
Faucet at 3 bay sink observed leaking. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the trash room area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024
